subject: Re: Warp not seeing all .tif files in a directory
body:
The file discovery procedure contains heuristics to make it work well with 
local storage and a network (Samba) share. However, I assumed it would be 
robust enough to at least not crash in different scenarios. I'll need to 
get myself an AWS instance and debug it.

We collect mostly with SerialEM and I haven't seen any issues related to 
the file names beside the lexicographic sorting. I agree the latter can be 
annoying, especially with EPU's names. Will look into using file creation 
timestamps as an alternative (however, those don't necessarily survive file 
movement).

Cheers,
Dimitry

On Tuesday, November 12, 2019 at 9:32:25 PM UTC+1, Andres Hernandez wrote:
>
> Hi Mike and Dimitry, I synced to a directory with aws then synced into the 
> WARP listening directory with python cp and it stopped crashing, it is 
> definitely an AWS s3 sync issue. The files are still listed out of order 
> and not found until I restart Warp, but I think that has to do with the way 
> SerialEM numbers the movies.
>
> On Sunday, July 15, 2018 at 11:33:21 AM UTC-7, mcia...@umich.edu wrote:
>>
>> Hi Dimitry, 
>>
>> We have noticed that the Warp directory being processed has ~2800 movies, 
>> but the total listing of movies in the "Overview" tab only totals 1,672. Is 
>> there a way to force the program to double check for the presence of 
>> additional files? I've tried stopping, exiting, restarting but it comes 
>> back to the same number.  Have you seen this before?
>>
>> Mike
>>
>
